title: Ace Attorney Investigations: Miles Edgeworth
creator: Shu Takumi
notes: Follows prosecutor Miles Edgeworth investigating crimes directly rather than in court. Five cases spanning international smuggling rings.

persons.name: Miles Edgeworth
persons.notes: Protagonist; brilliant prosecutor who investigates murders directly.

persons.name: Dick Gumshoe
persons.notes: Loyal detective who assists Edgeworth.

persons.name: Kay Faraday
persons.notes: Self-styled 'Great Thief' who assists Edgeworth.

persons.name: Shi-Long Lang
persons.notes: Interpol agent investigating smuggling.

persons.name: Quercus Alba
persons.notes: Ambassador and true mastermind of the smuggling ring.

persons.name: Jacques Portsman
persons.notes: Corrupt prosecutor; killer in Case 1.

persons.name: Buddy Faith
persons.notes: Detective killed by Portsman in Edgeworth's office.

persons.name: Mack Rell
persons.notes: Smuggler killed by Portsman.

persons.name: Cammy Meele
persons.notes: Flight attendant and smuggler; killer in Case 2.

persons.name: Akbey Hicks
persons.notes: Interpol agent killed by Cammy Meele on the flight.

persons.name: Oliver Deacon
persons.notes: Kidnapper in Case 3; real name Colin Devorae.

persons.name: Lance Amano
persons.notes: Staged his own kidnapping; killed Devorae.

persons.name: Colin Devorae
persons.notes: Former smuggler killed by Lance Amano.

persons.name: Calisto Yew
persons.notes: Defense attorney secretly working for the smuggling ring; killed Byrne Faraday.

persons.name: Byrne Faraday
persons.notes: Prosecutor and the original 'Yatagarasu'; killed by Calisto Yew.

persons.name: Manny Coachen
persons.notes: Smuggling lieutenant; killed by Quercus Alba.

persons.name: Ka-Shi Nou
persons.notes: Victim in Turnabout Ablaze; killed by Quercus Alba.

deaths.notes: Case 1 (Turnabout Visitor): Detective Faith killed in Edgeworth's office while Portsman searched for smuggling evidence.
Rell was a smuggler framed as the 'Yatagarasu'; killed by Portsman to silence him.
Case 2 (Turnabout Airlines): Interpol agent Hicks killed mid-flight to protect the smuggling ring.
Case 3 (The Kidnapped Turnabout): Devorae posed as kidnapper Oliver Deacon; killed by Lance Amano who staged his own kidnapping.
Case 4 (Turnabout Reminiscence): Flashback case; prosecutor Faraday was Kay's father and a Yatagarasu member, killed by mole Calisto Yew.
Case 5 (Turnabout Ablaze): Smuggling lieutenant killed by ambassador Alba to prevent exposure.
Case 5 (Turnabout Ablaze): Second victim killed by Alba during the climax.

title: Ace Attorney Investigations 2
creator: Shu Takumi
notes: Edgeworth investigates a conspiracy involving a secret 'prosecutor's trophy' and a self-declared emperor. Released only in Japan; fan-translated.

persons.name: Miles Edgeworth
persons.notes: Protagonist prosecutor.

persons.name: Dick Gumshoe
persons.notes: Detective assisting Edgeworth.

persons.name: Kay Faraday
persons.notes: Yatagarasu assistant.

persons.name: Sebastian Debeste
persons.notes: Prosecutor son of Blaise Debeste.

persons.name: Blaise Debeste
persons.notes: Chief Prosecutor and mastermind; manipulated the IS-7 incident.

persons.name: Simon Keyes
persons.notes: True mastermind; orchestrated murders out of revenge for being abandoned.

persons.name: Jill Crane
persons.notes: Victim in Case 1; connected to the smuggling world.

persons.name: Horace Knightley
persons.notes: Prisoner killer in Case 2; killed by Simon Keyes later.

persons.name: Sirhan Dogen
persons.notes: Blind assassin imprisoned in Rhiny Land; kills Patricia Roland.

persons.name: Ryouken Housekeeper
persons.notes: Prison warden killed in Case 2.

persons.name: Patricia Roland
persons.notes: Warden killed by Dogen; had murdered Knightley previously.

persons.name: Dane Gustavia
persons.notes: Pastry chef involved in the IS-7 incident; killed Bansai Ichiyanagi.

persons.name: Bansai Ichiyanagi
persons.notes: Former chief prosecutor killed by Gustavia in the IS-7 incident.

persons.name: Pierre Hoquet
persons.notes: Storyteller killed in Case 4.

persons.name: Marī Miwa
persons.notes: Killer in Case 4 (The Forgotten Turnabout).

persons.name: Zenitaro Tomitaro
persons.notes: Victim in Case 4.

persons.name: Teikun Ou
persons.notes: Self-proclaimed emperor; manipulated by Keyes.

persons.name: Sōta Sarushiro
persons.notes: Simon Keyes's alias; true final villain.

persons.name: Horace Knightley victim
persons.notes: Prisoner killed by Knightley in Case 2.

persons.name: Frank Sahwit
persons.notes: Prisoner in Case 2 (cameo from original game).

deaths.notes: Case 1 (Turnabout Inherited): Crane killed to prevent exposure of Debeste's past crimes.
Case 2 (The Imprisoned Turnabout): Murder committed in the prison yard.
Case 2: Prison warden secretly killed by Roland.
Case 2: Dogen kills Roland on behalf of Keyes's revenge scheme.
Case 4 (The Forgotten Turnabout): Storyteller killed during a memory-loss episode.
Case 4: Additional victim killed by the same perpetrator.
Case 5 (The Grand Turnabout) / IS-7 flashback: Former chief prosecutor killed; covered up by Blaise Debeste.
Case 5: Knightley eliminated by Simon Keyes (Sarushiro) as part of his revenge plot.

title: Professor Layton vs. Phoenix Wright: Ace Attorney
creator: Shu Takumi
notes: Crossover between the Professor Layton and Ace Attorney series. Set in the medieval town of Labyrinthia where magic and witch trials are real.

persons.name: Phoenix Wright
persons.notes: Defense attorney transported to Labyrinthia.

persons.name: Professor Hershel Layton
persons.notes: Gentleman puzzle-solver transported to Labyrinthia.

persons.name: Maya Fey
persons.notes: Phoenix's assistant; accused of witchcraft.

persons.name: Luke Triton
persons.notes: Layton's apprentice.

persons.name: Espella Cantabella
persons.notes: Young woman believed to be a great witch; catalyst of the story.

persons.name: Darklaw
persons.notes: Leader of the Shades enforcing the Story; real name Bezella.

persons.name: The Storyteller
persons.notes: Eldoon Cantabella; architect of Labyrinthia's false reality.

persons.name: Wordsmith
persons.notes: Scholar in Labyrinthia.

persons.name: Newton Belduke
persons.notes: Scholar killed in Labyrinthia; central murder in the story.

persons.name: Kira
persons.notes: Shade and killer of Belduke; working for Darklaw.

persons.name: Hilda Labrume
persons.notes: Victim in Labyrinthia; burned as a witch.

persons.name: Shades
persons.notes: Enforcers of the Storyteller's magic who carry out executions.

persons.name: Robbs and Muggs
persons.notes: Pair of comedic brigands encountered in Labyrinthia.

persons.name: Eldoon Cantabella
persons.notes: The Storyteller; created Labyrinthia as experiment; dies at end.

persons.name: Bezella
persons.notes: Darklaw's true identity; believed to be a great witch.

deaths.notes: Central murder mystery of the game; Belduke killed by Shade Kira to protect the Storyteller's secret.
Witch trial execution carried out in Labyrinthia under the Great Witch laws.
The Storyteller dies at the story's end; his death collapses the illusion sustaining Labyrinthia.
